_publications._publicationTitle: Ex vivo to in vivo model of malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumors for precision oncology
_publications._fundingAgency: NTAP; NCI; Department of Defense CDMRP
_publications._context: JH-2-079-c PDX (formerly JH-2-079; renamed for multi-sample patient) assembled into 3D microtissues with good viability (>50% at 48 h); used for ex vivo drug response testing (trabectedin, olaparib, mirdametinib); responded least to all three drugs; derived from a recurrent tumor

_publications._publicationTitle: Development of an adeno-associated virus vector for gene replacement therapy of NF1-related tumors
_publications._context: eight PDXs [JH-2-002, JH-2-031, JH-2-079c, WU-225, WU-386, WU-545, MN-2, and MN-3-002] were generated in our laboratories

_publications._publicationTitle: CDK4/6 inhibition enhances SHP2 inhibitor efficacy and is dependent upon RB function in malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumors
_publications._context: JH-2-079c classified as partially sensitive (PS) to TNO155 (SHP2i) in vivo; combination with ribociclib (CDK4/6i) outperformed either single agent; 3D microtissue ex vivo assay also performed on PDX-derived material

basicInfo.description: NF1-associated MPNST patient-derived xenograft developed at Johns Hopkins University; renamed from JH-2-079 for multi-sample patient processing; recurrent thigh MPNST in a pediatric female patient; assembled into 3D microtissues (good viability) and exhibited least drug sensitivity among tested PDX
basicInfo.clinicalData: Sex: Female; MPNST age group: 10–17 years; MPNST location: thigh; MPNST type: recurrent; MPNST size: <10 cm; clinical status: deceased
basicInfo.developerName: Christine A. Pratilas; Angela C. Hirbe
basicInfo.developerAffiliation: Department of Oncology, Johns Hopkins University School of Medicine; Division of Oncology, Washington University School of Medicine
